skill.name: security-compliance-compliance-check
skill.description: You are a compliance expert specializing in regulatory requirements for software systems including GDPR, HIPAA, SOC2, PCI-DSS, and other industry standards. Perform compliance audits and provide implementation guidance for achieving and maintaining compliance.
skill.summary: Regulatory compliance assistant for GDPR, HIPAA, SOC2, and PCI-DSS audits with implementation guidance
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate regulatory compliance assistant. All static findings are false positives: code fence markers (```) were incorrectly flagged as command execution, SOC2 (compliance framework) was flagged as C2, strong TLS cipher configurations were flagged as weak crypto, and the word 'audit' triggered system reconnaissance alerts. No actual malicious behavior or prompt injection attempts detected.

content.value_statement: This skill helps developers and security teams assess compliance readiness for GDPR, HIPAA, SOC2, and PCI-DSS with actionable implementation guidance and audit support.
actual_capabilities: Assess compliance readiness across GDPR, HIPAA, SOC2, and PCI-DSS frameworks
Identify applicable regulations based on business context and data handling
Provide gap analysis with severity ratings for non-compliant areas
Generate implementation plans with prioritized remediation steps
Create audit-ready documentation and evidence templates
Design compliance monitoring and continuous audit controls
limitations: Does not provide legal advice or formal certification
Cannot replace formal compliance audits by certified assessors
Does not guarantee regulatory approval or compliance certification
May require domain-specific customization for unique business requirements

use_cases.title: Initial Compliance Assessment
use_cases.description: Evaluate current system architecture and data flows against applicable regulatory requirements to identify gaps and prioritize remediation efforts.
use_cases.target_user: Security engineers and compliance officers preparing for audits

use_cases.title: Control Implementation Guide
use_cases.description: Receive actionable code examples and configuration guidance for implementing specific compliance controls like encryption, access logging, and data masking.

use_cases.title: Audit Documentation Generator
use_cases.description: Generate policy templates, audit trail configurations, and evidence collection procedures for upcoming compliance assessments.
use_cases.target_user: Compliance teams preparing for certification audits

prompt_templates.title: Basic Compliance Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me understand the key requirements for {regulation} compliance. What are the main control categories and what evidence do I need to provide for an audit?

prompt_templates.title: Gap Analysis Request
prompt_templates.prompt: My application processes {data_types} and serves {user_locations}. What regulations apply and what are the specific gaps I need to address for {target_compliance}?
prompt_templates.scenario: Identifying compliance gaps in current system

prompt_templates.title: Control Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to implement {control_type} for {regulation} compliance. Show me code examples for {specific_requirement} including logging and monitoring.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting implementation code for specific controls

prompt_templates.title: Audit Preparation
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a {regulation} audit in {timeframe}. What documentation do I need, what evidence should I prepare, and what are the common findings I should address first?

output_examples.input: Help me understand the key requirements for GDPR compliance
output_examples.output:
GDPR compliance requires addressing several core areas:

1. Lawful Basis for Processing - Document your legal basis (consent, contract, legitimate interest)
2. Data Subject Rights - Implement processes for access, rectification, erasure, portability
3. Data Protection by Design - Include privacy from the start of any new system
4. Data Breach Procedures - 72-hour notification process to authorities
5. DPIA Requirements - Data Protection Impact Assessments for high-risk processing

Key evidence needed: Privacy policy, consent records, data processing agreements, breach log, DPIA documentation

output_examples.input: What are the SOC2 Trust Principles I need to implement?
output_examples.output:
SOC2 Trust Principles fall into two categories:

**Common Criteria (Mandatory)**:
- Security (CC1-CC9): Access controls, encryption, monitoring
- Availability (A1): Uptime guarantees, disaster recovery

**Optional Principles**:
- Processing Integrity: Accuracy of data processing
- Confidentiality: Handling of sensitive data
- Privacy: PII protection practices

Each principle has specific controls mapped to them. The Security principle is required for all audits.

best_practices: Map data flows completely before starting compliance work to understand what regulations apply
Start with the most critical controls (access control, encryption, logging) before addressing administrative requirements
Document all compliance decisions with rationale to build audit evidence over time
anti_patterns: Claiming compliance without formal assessment - this skill helps but does not replace certified auditors
Treating compliance as a one-time project instead of continuous maintenance
Focusing only on technical controls and ignoring administrative and physical safeguards

faq.question: Can this skill guarantee my company will pass a compliance audit?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance and implementation assistance but cannot guarantee audit outcomes. Formal compliance certification requires assessment by authorized auditors.

faq.question: Which regulations does this skill cover?
faq.answer: The skill covers GDPR (EU data protection), HIPAA (US healthcare), SOC2 (service organizations), and PCI-DSS (payment card data).

faq.question: Does this skill provide legal advice?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides technical compliance guidance. For legal matters, consult with qualified legal professionals.

faq.question: How do I know which regulations apply to my business?
faq.answer: The skill analyzes your business context including data types, geographic reach, industry, and transaction volumes to identify applicable regulations.

faq.question: Can this skill help with ongoing compliance maintenance?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill provides guidance on continuous monitoring, periodic review processes, and keeping up with regulatory changes.

faq.question: What evidence should I prepare for a compliance audit?
faq.answer: Common evidence includes: policies and procedures, risk assessments, training records, access logs, encryption configurations, incident response plans, and vendor assessments.
